# Requirements Traceability Matrix

<!-- GENERATED by `npm run traceability` from REQUIREMENTS.md + the test suite. DO NOT EDIT BY HAND.
     A CI step regenerates this and fails if it is stale, so it can never drift from reality. -->

**x402-spendguard `v0.2.1`** ‚Äî 53 verified requirements ¬∑ 58 tests ¬∑ 58 requirement‚Üítest checks ¬∑ **53/53 verified** (58 passed ¬∑ 0 failed ¬∑ 0 skipped ¬∑ 0 missing).

Each requirement is verified by the named test(s). Their existence is *enforced* by
`test/traceability.test.ts` (the build fails if a named test is missing); their pass/fail status
here is from the suite run. **Verified** = every named test exists and passed. A test may verify
more than one requirement, so *checks* ‚â• *tests*.

| Requirement | Description | Threat | Verified by | Status |
|-------------|-------------|--------|-------------|--------|
| **HALT-01** | When `policy.halt` is true, `evaluate()` returns **deny** for every input, including otherwise-valid payments. | T13 | `halt-denies-valid` | ‚úÖ passed |
| **ALLOW-01** | If the allowlist is non-empty and the payment's `payTo` is not present, **deny**. | T1 | `allowlist-blocks-unlisted` | ‚úÖ passed |
| **ALLOW-02** | The allowlist is keyed on **(address, chain)**; the address is compared case-insensitively; the chain is not normalized away. | T1 | `allowlist-same-address-wrong-chain-denied` | ‚úÖ passed |
| **ALLOW-03** | An **empty** allowlist denies all payments (secure by default ‚Äî the user opts destinations *in*, rather than opting danger *out*). | T1 | `empty-allowlist-denies-all` | ‚úÖ passed |
| **BIND-01** | For scheme `exact`: **deny** unless the signed `value` equals the challenge `amount` exactly (rejects both over- and under-payment; note v1‚Ä¶ | T2, T5 | `bind-rejects-overpayment` | ‚úÖ passed |
| **BIND-02** | **Deny** unless the signed `to` equals the challenge `payTo` (after canonicalization). | T1 | `bind-rejects-recipient-swap` | ‚úÖ passed |
| **BIND-03** | **Deny** unless `validBefore ‚â§ now + min(server maxTimeoutSeconds, policy maxAuthLifetimeSeconds) + skew`. The challenge's `maxTimeoutSeconds` is‚Ä¶ | T6 | `bind-rejects-long-lived-auth` | ‚úÖ passed |
| **BIND-04** | **Deny** unless the signed asset (`verifyingContract`) and `chainId` match the challenge's declared asset and network. | T7 | `bind-rejects-asset-mismatch` | ‚úÖ passed |
| **BIND-05** | A non-`exact` scheme (e.g. `upto`, `auth-capture`) is **denied** with a stable reason. (`upto` is deferred to v2; see note.) | T2 | `nonexact-scheme-denied` | ‚úÖ passed |
| **SCOPE-01** | A payment whose authorization form is not `eip3009-evm` (e.g. Solana/SVM) is **denied at parse** with a stable reason. v1 is EVM-only (D-017). | T2 | `nonevm-form-denied` | ‚úÖ passed |
| **CAP-01** | Per-request: if `amount > cap[(asset, chain)]`, **deny**. | T2 | `cap-per-request-boundary` | ‚úÖ passed |
| **CAP-02** | Per-domain cumulative: if `domainSpent + amount > perDomainCap`, **deny**. | T3 | `cap-per-domain` | ‚úÖ passed |
| **CAP-03** | Global cumulative: if `globalSpent + amount > globalCap`, **deny**. | T4 | `cap-global` | ‚úÖ passed |
| **CAP-04** | Amounts of differing `(asset, chain)` are **never** summed into one cap. | T7 | `cap-no-cross-asset-sum` | ‚úÖ passed |
| **CAP-05** | A payment in an `(asset, chain)` with **no configured cap** is **denied** (missing cap = deny, fail-closed). | T2, T7 | `cap-asset-unconfigured` | ‚úÖ passed |
| **MONEY-01** | All amounts are integer smallest-units (`bigint`); the parser rejects negative, non-integer, `NaN`/`Infinity`, and out-of-decimal-range values; **no‚Ä¶ | T2, T7 | `money-rejects-malformed`, `money-precision-exact` | ‚úÖ passed |
| **FAIL-01** | Any thrown exception on the enforcement path results in **deny**. | T8 | `throwing-check-denies` | ‚úÖ passed |
| **FAIL-02** | A missing or malformed field in the challenge or payment results in **deny** ‚Äî never a skipped check. | T8 | `malformed-challenge-denies` | ‚úÖ passed |
| **PARSE-01** | Malformed input is denied with a **specific parse reason code** (e.g. `parse.amount_negative`, `scheme.unsupported`), never the generic‚Ä¶ | T8 | `parse-failure-specific-reason` | ‚úÖ passed |
| **FAIL-03** | An audit/log write failure does **not** flip an allow to a deny or a deny to an allow; but a failure to durably record a *spend* before the payment‚Ä¶ | T8, T10 | `audit-failure-preserves-decision`, `spend-record-failure-denies` | ‚úÖ passed |
| **ACCT-01** | Spend is recorded **write-ahead**, before the payment is released; a crash between record and settlement must never *under-count*. | T10 | `crash-between-record-and-settle-does-not-undercount` | ‚úÖ passed |
| **ACCT-02** | **Within a single guard instance/process,** evaluation is serialized (single-writer): two concurrent `authorize()` calls cannot both pass a cap they‚Ä¶ | T9 | `concurrent-payments-cannot-both-pass` | ‚úÖ passed |
| **ACCT-03** | Spend state is durable across a process restart. | T3, T4 | `state-survives-restart` | ‚úÖ passed |
| **CLOCK-01** | A **backward** clock jump must never reset a spend window or un-count spend ‚Äî time is viewed monotonically (`max(now, lastSeen)`). | T9 | `clock-anomaly-fails-closed` | ‚úÖ passed |
| **ACCT-05** | A spend store shared across processes is serialized (cross-process single-writer): two processes cannot both pass a cap they jointly exceed. | T9 | `cross-process-cannot-both-pass` | ‚úÖ passed |

| **PRIV-02** | The decision log never contains the signed authorization or the payment header (both are bearer capabilities). | T11 | `log-never-contains-signature` | ‚úÖ passed |
| **PRIV-03** | No telemetry. Absent, not opt-out. | T12 | `no-telemetry-calls` | ‚úÖ passed |
